event_preview: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya is a 4.657 km (2.894 mi) permanent road course in Montmeló, 20 km northeast of Barcelona, featuring 14 turns across a technical and physically demanding lap. The 4 Hours of Barcelona is the opening round of the 2026 European Le Mans Series season and the sixth consecutive running of the event at this venue, contested over a four-hour window across three classes — LMP2, LMP3 and LMGT3. A total of 47 cars are entered: 23 Oreca 07 LMP2s split across Pro and Pro/Am, 10 LMP3s and 14 LMGT3s. The long front straight leads into the braking zone at Turn 1 — the circuit's primary overtaking point — while the sustained right-hand arc of Turn 3 places prolonged load on left-side tyres, making tyre management a defining strategic factor across four hours. Last year's overall victory went to AF Corse's trio of François Perrodo, Matthieu Vaxivière and Alessio Rovera, with IDEC Sport's LMP2 class win marking the first time a woman — Jamie Chadwick — had won the LMP2 class outright in ELMS history. The all-time LMP2 qualifying lap record at Barcelona belongs to Reshad de Gerus, who turned a 1:27.664 in the No. 30 Duqueine Team Oreca to claim pole position at the 2025 4 Hours of Barcelona.
